evaluation.manager_summary: Catherine called with ambivalence about whether she needed help, initially minimizing her drinking pattern. Agent built excellent rapport, gathered complete clinical and insurance information, and skillfully reframed her objection around functional impact rather than labels. Caller moved from resistance to acceptance and agreed to callback within two hours for admissions coordination and outpatient track discussion.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent acknowledged caller's ambivalence immediately, normalized uncertainty, matched emotional register, used caller's name, and explicitly removed pressure. Excellent trust-building in the critical opening moments.

Agent collected carrier name, member ID, and group number efficiently. Framed verification as part of the support process. Did not explicitly mention plan type, though this may have been captured in the callback process mentioned.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Consider capturing plan type (PPO/HMO) during initial collection to reduce friction later.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent systematically gathered substance type, frequency, escalation pattern, functional impact, and prior treatment attempts without crossing into diagnostic territory. Excellent calibrated questioning that surfaces severity indicators naturally.

Agent correctly identified this as a same-week but not same-day need based on functional impairment and escalation pattern. Appropriate timeframe set (callback within two hours, outpatient track suggested). No crisis indicators present or missed.
Caller is the patient themselves. Dimension does not apply.

Agent acknowledged the core objection ('I'm not an alcoholic') without labeling or countering defensively, then reframed beautifully around functional impact rather than identity. Caller moved from resistance to acceptance organically.

Agent set specific next step (callback from admissions coordinator), clear timing (within two hours, by noon), clear ownership (benefits team and admissions coordinator), and level of care suggestion (outpatient track). Caller confirmed understanding.

Agent maintained perfect compliance boundaries: described patterns without diagnosing, normalized treatment-seeking, reframed around functional impact, and suggested level of care appropriately. No outcome guarantees, no diagnostic labels, no unauthorized clinical statements.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm glad your sister encouraged you to reach out, and I'm glad you followed through. There's no pressure here — I'm just going to ask a few questions and we can figure out together

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I appreciate you sharing that. Catherine, I want to reflect something back to you — not to label it, just so you can hear it.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And Catherine — calling was the right thing to do.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Let me ask a few more questions. Do you have insurance?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. Meridian Care. Member ID is BP62919803, group GRP-560846.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Thank you. And have you ever tried to cut back on your own?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I hear you. Can I ask — how many drinks is 'unwinding' for you on a typical evening?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And how often are the really bad days?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And have you ever tried to cut back on your own?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Rough. I've been calling in late to work a few times.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'll have our benefits team verify your coverage and an admissions coordinator call you back within two hours. They'll talk through your options — which based on what you've described would likely sta

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I drink, but it's not like I'm an alcoholic or anything. I just drink after work to unwind. Everyone does that.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: What you're describing — four to seven drinks most evenings, increasingly difficult mornings, calling in late, your sister noticing a change — those are patterns that tend to escalate without support.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: When you put it like that... yeah, it is.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'll have our benefits team verify your coverage and an admissions coordinator call you back within two hours. They'll talk through your options — which based on what you've described would likely sta

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Good. You'll hear from us by noon.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I want to reflect something back to you — not to label it, just so you can hear it. What you're describing — four to seven drinks most evenings, increasingly difficult mornings, calling in late, your

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That pattern — trying and returning — is really common and it's one of the things treatment specifically addresses.
